expressions.english: good idea
expressions.notes:
좋다 = to be good, likable; to be desirable; to be nice; to like
좋은 is the adjective form of 좋다.

expressions.english: to remember; to come to mind
to think again
to think cautiously
expressions.notes:
신중하다 = to be cautious
신중히 is an adverb form of 신중하다.

expressions.english: to think lightly
expressions.notes:
가볍다 = to be light
가볍게 is an adverb form of 가볍다.

expressions.english: to think carefully
organize one's thoughts
expressions.notes: 정리하다 = to organize; to tidy things up

expressions.english: Think carefully.
I will think about it.
The more I think about it, the more upset I get.
I have never thought like this before.
expressions.notes: 이렇게 = like this, in this manner; so +adjective

expressions.english: Anyone who remembers the correct answer, please raise your hand.
expressions.notes:
답 = answer; correct answer; reply
손 = hand
들다 = to raise; to pick (something) up

expressions.korean: 다시 생각해 봤는데, 저는 안 갈래요.
expressions.english: On second thought, I'm not going to go.
Since it's an important matter, you should think (about it) carefully.
expressions.notes:
중요하다 = to be important
중요한 is the adjective form of 중요하다.
-(이)니까 = since/because + S + be + N

expressions.english: Have you given it some thought?
expressions.notes: -에 대해서 = about; regarding + N; concerning + N

expressions.korean: 아무리 생각해도 그건 틀린 것 같아요.
expressions.english: No matter how much I think about it, I still think it's wrong.
expressions.notes:
아무리 -아/어/이어도 = no matter how + adjective + S + V
틀리다 = to be wrong

expressions.korean: 지금 드는 생각을 솔직하게 말해 보세요.
expressions.english: Please tell me honestly what comes to your mind.
expressions.notes:
들다 = to enter; to be categorized as; to be in
솔직하다 = to be honest
솔직하게 is the adverb form of 솔직하다.

expressions.korean: 열쇠를 어디에 두었는지 생각이 안 나요.
expressions.english: I don't remember where I put my key.
expressions.notes:
열쇠 = key
어디 = where; somewhere
두다 = to leave (something) at a place

expressions.korean: 곰곰이 생각해 봤는데, 이게 좋을 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I have given it a lot of thought and I think this will be good.
expressions.notes:
이게 = this (subject)
이게 is the contracted form of 이것이.

expressions.korean: 지금 다시 생각해 보면, 학교 다닐 때가 좋았던 것 같아요.
expressions.english: Come to think of it, I think I was happy when I was going to school.
expressions.notes:
학교 = school
다니다 = to go, attend
